rationale: The actual thread shows maintainers still investigating (yenuo26 asks Flink-ddd to check it, Flink-ddd asks for a full CI trigger to verify) rather than a settled 'already fixed on main, close' resolution — both candidates overstate confidence by recommending closure, but X hedges more appropriately ('please confirm CI was on right commit, re-trigger if needed') while Y asserts a definitive fix with unverifiable specifics (exact PR title, merge date '2026-07-05', three named regression tests, specific admission-guard line numbers) that read as fabricated embellishment beyond what the issue or thread supports. Both correctly ground the core diagnosis in the same async_omni.py guard code tied to PR #4834, matching the thread's attribution, but Y's excess invented detail hurts its grounding credibility more than it helps completeness.
